title: Accusé de réception chypriote de l'instrument français : note verbale et lettre (avec, sur la note verbale, tampon arrivée le 4 février 1983 à l'ambassade de France)

pages.text:
REPUBLIC

OF CYPRUS

MINISTRY OF FOREIGN AFFAIRS

No 848/69

NOTE VERBALE

....

The Winistry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Cyprus presents its compliments to the Embassy of

France and has the honour to acknowledge receipt of the latter's Note 24/AL of 28 January 1983, together with

the

letter from H.E. Ir. Claude Cheysson, Kinister of

External Relations, addressed to H.li. the Minister of

Foreign Affairs, concerning the Convention for the

Avoidance of Double Taxation between the two countries.

A corresponding letter from H.i. lir. Nicos A.

Rolandis to I.i. Wr. Claude Cheysson is enclosed herewith and it will be appreciatedif it is delivered to its destination.

The Cyprus constitutional requirements for the entry into force of the above Convention have also be fulfílled as notified by the Ministry's Note 848 of 13 August 1982. It will therefore enter into force on 1 April 1983, as stipulated by article 31 thereof.

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Cyprus avails itself of this opportunity to rener to the Embassy of France the assurances of its highest consideration.

The Embassy of France,

NICOSIA

i OF CyPRUS

4-2.83

9

MINISTRY OF FOREIGN AFFAIRS

MINISTER'S OFFICE

Ref. 848/69

Nicosia 3rd

February 1983

My dies

I have the honour to refer to your letter of 19

January 1983 concerning the Convention between the

Government of the Republic of Cyprus and the Government of the Republic of France for the Avoidance of Double

Taxation and the Prevention of Fiscal Evasion with

Respect to Taxes on Income and on Capital you that the Cyprus constitutional requirements for the entry into force of the Convention have also been fulfilled.

The said Convention will therefore entor into f'orce on 1 April 1983, as stipulated in article 31 thereof.

Accept, dear Mr. Minister,

the assurances

of my

highest consideration.

1. 92.1

N. A. Rolandis

Minister

of Foreign Affairs

of the Republic of Cyprus

His

Excellency

Mr. Claude

Cheyason,

Minister

of External

paris,

France.

Relations,
